text: When fanning out read-only reviewers in parallel with a mutating tester over shared state files, warn EVERY reader about transient mutations (or sequence them) — otherwise reviewers report probe-injected state as live findings.

Extension messages sent with deliverAs:"nextTurn" (graph-first nudge, impact-trace) are invisible to the agent within the same run — verify those branches behaviorally (e.g. the 2nd structure grep blocking proves the 1st nudged) or by jiti-loading the extension with a stub pi that records sendMessage.

When rebuilding ~/.pi/agent's graphify graph with Gemini, use token_budget≈12000 plus deep_mode=True and deterministic INFERRED mentions bridge edges; the default 60k budget yields sparse edges and a ~0.2–0.3 giant fraction.

The graphify semantic cache lives inside graphify-out/cache, so deleting graphify-out forces a full paid LLM re-extraction — before rebuilding, check whether the old graph.json is recoverable from a pre-untrack git commit (git show <sha>:graphify-out/graph.json).

When acting on a scout's duplication audit, independently grep the whole directory for the shared pattern before treating the enumerated list as complete — the scout's "7 scripts" jiti-bootstrap list missed test-config-paths.mjs which shared the exact idiom.
